Dear Tom,

Thank you very much for raising your concern about Hong Kong’s current situation, in which your committee proposed to grant full U.K. citizenships to the British Hong Kong people in the effort to reassure us in such chaotic time.

I personally am enormously grateful for that. For your committee to even consider the same is a huge leap of faith for us. The mere knowledge that that we are not alone under the continuous suppression of the Chinese tyranny, has given us hope. A great deal of hope. It is because, at times, we do feel small, alone, unheard, misunderstood and frustrated, especially in times like this.

I understand the implementation of such proposed policy is currently at its premature stage and the legislative process, if any, will be in matter of years.

However, let me reassure you, the Hong Kong people who are tirelessly voicing their opinions, have the deepest desire to be a part of a community that enjoy freedom and rights with minimal restrictions like the one enjoyed by the British people.

I sincerely appreciate your support, time and effort in this matter. We’d be enormously grateful for your effort to try your best to make this happen for us.

Dear British Citizens,

Most people who were born in Hong Kong before 1997 hold British National (Overseas) passport (BNO), which means they can only stay in the UK for up to 6 months. British National Overseas citizens are the only few nationalities with no right of abode in their own country.

As you may see on the news, China has breached the Sino-British Joint Declaration repeatedly, putting the lives of Hong Kong people at risk. We have no choice but to suffer under the Chinese rule.

We (British National Overseas citizens) are quality British. We can make Britain great again with the following facts:
1. Hong Kong people are highly educated. The majority of the young people received tertiary education.
2. GDP per capital of Hong Kong is comparable to that of UK, which ranks rank 15th in the world according to the International Monetary Fund (2018).
3. The average Hongkonger owns USD$245,000, which ranks 14th in the world according to the Global Wealth Report 2018 published by the Credit Suisse Research Institute.
4. Hong Kong people share common value with British.
5. Hong Kong people are pragmatic, industrious. We want to contribute to society.
6. We respect the rule of law because this is taught by the British before 1997.

As Tom Tugendhat (MP) put forward: “The UK had obligations to Hong Kong citizens before 1997, and the extension of overseas citizenship, which is in many ways a second-tier citizenship, was a mistake, and I think it’s one that should be corrected. At a time when there are clearly tensions in Hong Kong, the UK could reassure many Hong Kong citizens that their existing rights are recognised by the UK, and they are valued.”

Please help Hong Kong by signing this petition:
https://petition.parliament.uk/petitions/244402

You help is much appreciated.

Many thanks,
British National Overseas citizens
